text.title: Solo Testimonial Extraction Script Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding solo-testimonial-extraction-script. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Outreach MUST ask exactly 3 questions: hesitation-before, outcome-after, who-NOT-to-recommend. Adding a 4th dilutes; removing the anti-recommendation collapses to generic praise.
rule.rationale: The anti-recommendation question is the load-bearing one; it produces specific, credible language.

rule.statement: The 'who would you NOT recommend this to' question MUST be included. Skipping it produces vague praise.
rule.rationale: Generic praise reads as marketing copy; specific scope ('not for solo founders below $1k MRR') reads as credible.

rule.statement: Customer's exact words MUST be preserved. AI-rewriting the response is forbidden; light editing for typos only.
rule.rationale: Customers detect ghostwriting; verbatim quotes carry social proof, rewrites destroy it.

rule.statement: Outreach MUST request explicit consent to publish quote + name + role. Implicit consent is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without explicit consent, publication is a trust violation regardless of legality.

rule.statement: Output Testimonial JSON MUST contain a numeric outcome metric (X% / Y hours / $Z). Vague outcomes ('great experience') are flagged.
